column6: Please Add the following Headword as indicated in the window display: " ˚maya ". According to the AP spelling, this compound will appear as: "kiraṇamaya". This Headword Deserves to be listed as a Searchable Headword within the Hierarchy, in order to Fully Honor and Pay Resect to AP57.

column7: Typo – "Print Change". Please note that the technical term with a hyphen introduces an artificial break in a single concept; additionally, the spelling of the sibilant and nasal requires consistency
Typo – Please note that the Standard IAST dictionary convention favors using ṅ (saṅkaraḥ) before a velar consonant rather than the raw anusvāra dot ṃ (saṃkaraḥ).
column8: srhodes@snowcrest.net:No change required. It is rather other way round. AP prefers M over vargapaYcama.

column7: Typo – In the context of this epic stanza describing an action, it must be the masculine nominative singular form matching the subject, or a neuter attribute depending on the internal sandhi of the verse quarter. Standard lexicography tracks it as jātasaṃbhramaḥ
column8: srhodes@snowcrest.net:No change required. See the full stanza. https://sa.wikisource.org/wiki/पृष्ठम्:किरातार्जुनीयम्_(मल्लिनाथव्याख्योपेतम्).pdf/१३१

column7: Typo – While phonetic sandhi blends the d of kecit into vadanti, they are independent grammatical units (a pronoun and a verb). Separating the verb makes the text scannable for Western Sanskrit students.
